1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**

540→ # Check if target branch has a worktree (or a worktree at expected path) 541→ lookup = find_worktree_for_branch_or_path(ctx, repo, target_branch, worktrees) 542→ if lookup.path is not None: 543→ # Worktree exists (checkout will be handled by orchestrator if needed) 544→ return target_branch, False 545→ 546→ # No worktree found - allocate a slot 547→ _worktree_path, already_existed = ensure_branch_has_worktree( 548→ ctx, repo, branch_name=target_branch, no_slot=False, force=False 549→ ) 550→ was_created = not already_existed 551→ return target_branch, was_created 552→ 553→ 554→def resolve_down_navigation( 555→ ctx: ErkContext, 556→ *, 557→ repo: RepoContext, 558→ current_branch: str, 559→ worktrees: list[WorktreeInfo], 560→ trunk_branch: str | None, 561→) -> tuple[str, bool]: 562→ """Resolve --down navigation to determine target branch name. 563→ 564→ Args: 565→ ctx: Erk context 566→ repo: Repository context 567→ current_branch: Current branch name 568→ worktrees: List of worktrees from git_ops.worktree.list_worktrees() 569→ trunk_branch: Configured trunk branch name, or None for auto-detection

600→ else: 601→ # Parent is not trunk, check if it has a worktree (or one at expected path) 602→ lookup = find_worktree_for_branch_or_path(ctx, repo, parent_branch, worktrees) 603→ if lookup.path is not None: 604→ # Worktree exists (checkout will be handled by orchestrator if needed) 605→ return parent_branch, False 606→ 607→ # No worktree found - allocate a slot 608→ _worktree_path, already_existed = ensure_branch_has_worktree( 609→ ctx, repo, branch_name=parent_branch, no_slot=False, force=False 610→ ) 611→ was_created = not already_existed 612→ return parent_branch, was_created 613→ 614→ 615→@dataclass(frozen=True) 616→class NavigationResult: 617→ """Result of navigation resolution.""" 618→ 619→ target_path: Path 620→ target_name: str 621→ was_created: bool 622→ is_root: bool 623→ 624→ 625→@dataclass(frozen=True) 626→class WorktreeLookupResult: 627→ """Result of looking up a worktree by branch or expected path.""" 628→ 629→ path: Path | None

Perfect. Now I have all the information needed. Let me classify the PR feedback according to the skill instructions:
## Analysis
Based on the skill instructions and the fetched data for PR 9049:
**PR Details:** - PR Number: 9049 - Title: "Fix slot reuse in erk up/down navigation" - Files Modified: 4 files (navigation_helpers.py, land_cmd.py, test_down.py, test_up.py) - No file restructuring detected (only modifications to existing files)
**Review Threads Analysis:**
1. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5zA6d2** (line 549, navigation_helpers.py): - Author: github-actions (bot) - Issue: Unnecessary intermediate variable with boolean inversion - The variable `was_created = not already_existed` is flagged as unnecessary - This is NOT pre-existing (the PR doesn't involve file restructuring, and the pattern is being introduced/modified in this PR) - Classification: **actionable** (code simplification suggestion) - Complexity: **local** (single line at specified location)
2. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5zA6hg** (line 609, navigation_helpers.py): - Author: github-actions (bot) - Issue: Same boolean inversion pattern repeated - Not pre-existing (same reasoning as above) - Classification: **actionable** (code simplification suggestion) - Complexity: **local** (single line at specified location)
**Discussion Comments Analysis:** - All discussion comments are informational (status updates, workflow notifications, bot-generated reviews that document findings rather than requesting action) - These include: submission queued, implementation started, review summaries - Count: 7 informational discussion comments
